client.h(3) Library Functions Manual client.h(3)

NAME

client.h - Per-client state management and lifecycle orchestration.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct client_manager_t
Global client manager structure for server-side client coordination.

Typedefs


typedef struct server_context_t server_context_t

Functions


client_info_t * add_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, socket_t socket, const char *client_ip, int port)
client_info_t * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, const char *client_ip, bool start_threads)
Register a WebRTC client with the server. int start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t *server_ctx, const char *client_id)
Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization. int remove_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, const char *client_id)
client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *client_id)
Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t socket)
Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. void cleanup_client_media_buffers (client_info_t *client)
void cleanup_client_packet_queues (client_info_t *client)
void * client_receive_thread (void *arg)
void stop_client_threads (client_info_t *client)
int process_encrypted_packet (client_info_t *client, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len, uint32_t *sender_id)
void process_decrypted_packet (client_info_t *client, packet_type_t type, void *data, size_t len)
void initialize_client_info (client_info_t *client)

Variables


client_manager_t g_client_manager
Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock
Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager. bool g_client_manager_rwlock_initialized

Detailed Description

Per-client state management and lifecycle orchestration.

This header provides server-specific client management functions. The client_info_t structure and network logging macros are defined in lib/network/client.h.

Definition in file src/server/client.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct server_context_t server_context_t

Definition at line 16 of file src/server/client.h.

Function Documentation

client_info_t * add_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, socket_t socket, const char * client_ip, int port)

Definition at line 619 of file src/server/client.c.

619                                                                                                           {
620   log_info("[TCP_ADD_CLIENT] ENTER: client_ip=%s, port=%d", client_ip, port);
621   // Find empty slot WITHOUT holding the global lock
622   // We'll re-verify under lock after allocations complete
623   int slot = -1;
624   int existing_count = 0;
625   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
626     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
627       slot = i; // Take first available slot
628     }
629     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
630       existing_count++;
631     }
632   }
633 
634   // Quick pre-check before expensive allocations
635   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_CHECK: existing_count=%d, max_clients=%d, slot=%d", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients),
636            slot);
637   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients) || slot == -1) {
638     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Maximum client limit reached\n";
639     ssize_t send_result = socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
640     if (send_result < 0) {
641       log_warn("Failed to send rejection message to client: %s", SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
642     }
643     return NULL;
644   }
645 
646   // NOW acquire the lock early for generating unique client name
647   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRE_START");
648   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
649   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED");
650 
651   // Generate unique client ID (noun only, without counter or port)
652   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
653   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_START");
654   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
655     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
656     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID");
657     return NULL;
658   }
659   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_DONE: id=%s", new_client_id);
660 
661   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASE_START");
662   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
663   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASED");
664 
665   // DO EXPENSIVE ALLOCATIONS OUTSIDE THE LOCK
666   // This prevents blocking frame processing during client initialization
667   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_START: About to create video frame buffer for client_id=%s", new_client_id);
668   video_frame_buffer_t *incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
669   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_DONE: Video buffer creation returned %p", (void *)incoming_video_buffer);
670   if (!incoming_video_buffer) {
671     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
672     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
673     return NULL;
674   }
675 
676   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_DONE: About to create audio buffer");
677   audio_ring_buffer_t *incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
678   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_DONE: Audio buffer created");
679   if (!incoming_audio_buffer) {
680     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
681     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
682     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
683     return NULL;
684   }
685 
686   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_START: Creating audio queue");
687   packet_queue_t *audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
688   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_DONE");
689   if (!audio_queue) {
690     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for client");
691     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
692     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
693     return NULL;
694   }
695 
696   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_START: Creating outgoing video buffer");
697   video_frame_buffer_t *outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
698   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_DONE");
699   if (!outgoing_video_buffer) {
700     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for client");
701     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
702     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
703     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
704     return NULL;
705   }
706 
707   void *send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, 64, void *);
708   if (!send_buffer) {
709     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
710     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
711     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
712     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
713     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
714     return NULL;
715   }
716 
717   // NOW acquire the lock for the critical section: slot assignment + registration
718   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_START: About to acquire client manager write lock");
719   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
720   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED: Client manager lock acquired");
721   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_RECHECK: About to check if slot is still available");
722 
723   // Re-check slot availability under lock (another thread might have taken it)
724   if (g_client_manager.clients[slot].client_id[0] != '\0') {
725     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
726     SAFE_FREE(send_buffer);
727     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
728     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
729     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
730     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
731 
732     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Slot reassigned, try again\n";
733     socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
734     return NULL;
735   }
736 
737   // Now we have exclusive access to the slot - do the actual registration
738   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
739   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
740 
741   // Initialize codec capabilities to zero (no codecs supported until CLIENT_CAPABILITIES received)
742   client->codec_capabilities_video = 0;
743   client->codec_capabilities_audio = 0;
744 
745   client->socket = socket;
746   client->is_tcp_client = true;
747   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
748   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
749   client->port = port;
750   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
751   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for cleanup
752   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
753   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0);
754   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);
755   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
756 
757   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
758   client->crypto_initialized = false;
759 
760   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
761   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
762   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
763 
764   // Assign pre-allocated buffers
765   client->incoming_video_buffer = incoming_video_buffer;
766   client->incoming_audio_buffer = incoming_audio_buffer;
767   client->audio_queue = audio_queue;
768   client->outgoing_video_buffer = outgoing_video_buffer;
769   client->send_buffer = send_buffer;
770   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
771 
772   // Generate unique noun-based client name with transport type and port
773   // Note: We're holding the write lock here, so it's safe to iterate existing clients
774   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME");
775   if (generate_client_name(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), g_client_manager.clients_by_id, port,
776                            true /* is_tcp */) != 0) {
777     // Fallback to numeric name if generation fails
778     safe_snprintf(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), "client_%u (tcp:%d)", new_client_id, port);
779   }
780   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME: display_name=%s", client->display_name);
781 
782   // Register client with named debug system using the generated name
783   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT(client, client->display_name, NULL);
784 
785   log_info("Added new client %s from %s:%d (socket=%d, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, port, socket, slot);
786   log_debug("Client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d, socket=%d", new_client_id, slot, socket);
787 
788   // Register socket with tcp_server
789   asciichat_error_t reg_result = tcp_server_add_client(server_ctx->tcp_server, socket, client);
790   if (reg_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
791     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to register client socket with tcp_server");
792     log_error("Failed to register client %s socket with tcp_server", new_client_id);
793     // Don't unlock here - error_cleanup will do it
794     goto error_cleanup;
795   }
796 
797   g_client_manager.client_count++;
798   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added client_id=%s to slot %d)", g_client_manager.client_count,
799             new_client_id, slot);
800 
801   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
802   log_debug("Added client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
803 
804   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
805 
806   // Configure socket OUTSIDE lock
807   configure_client_socket(socket, new_client_id);
808 
809   // Initialize mutexes OUTSIDE lock BEFORE registering them
810   // register_client_info_atomics needs these mutexes to be initialized first
811   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
812     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
813     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
814     return NULL;
815   }
816 
817   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
818     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
819     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
820     return NULL;
821   }
822 
823   // Register all atomic fields for debug tracking - DISABLED to prevent deadlock
824   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() call
825   // NAMED_REGISTER which tries to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock
826   // The debug_sync thread holds READ lock while printing, causing reader-writer deadlock
827   // These are debug-only features not critical for functionality
828   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
829   // register_client_info_atomics(client);
830   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
831 
832   // Audio buffer atomics registration disabled - same deadlock reason
833   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_START: client=%p", (void *)client);
834   // if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
835   //   audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics(client->incoming_audio_buffer, new_client_id);
836   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_DONE");
837   // } else {
838   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_SKIPPED: incoming_audio_buffer is NULL");
839   // }
840 
841   // Register with audio mixer OUTSIDE lock
842   // CRITICAL: Do this BEFORE crypto handshake to avoid deadlock with mixer thread
843   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_START: g_audio_mixer=%p, client->incoming_audio_buffer=%p", (void *)g_audio_mixer,
844            (void *)client->incoming_audio_buffer);
845   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
846     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_CALLING: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
847     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
848       log_warn("Failed to add client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
849     } else {
850       log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SUCCESS: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
851 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
852       log_debug("Added client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
853 #endif
854     }
855   } else {
856     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SKIPPED: conditions not met");
857   }
858   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_DONE");
859 
860   // Create the client's persistent transport early (before handshake).
861   // It starts with NULL crypto; server_crypto_handshake() sets crypto_ctx after success.
862   client->transport = acip_tcp_transport_create(new_client_id, socket, NULL);
863   if (!client->transport) {
864     log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for client %s", new_client_id);
865     if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
866       log_error("Failed to remove client after transport creation failure");
867     }
868     return NULL;
869   }
870   log_debug("Created ACIP transport for client %s (crypto pending handshake)", new_client_id);
871 
872   // Perform crypto handshake before starting threads.
873   // This ensures the handshake uses the socket directly without interference from receive thread.
874   // The handshake uses client->transport and sets its crypto_ctx on success.
875   log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_START: About to call server_crypto_init()");
876   if (server_crypto_init() == 0) {
877     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_DONE: server_crypto_init() returned 0");
878     // Set timeout for crypto handshake to prevent indefinite blocking
879     // This prevents clients from connecting but never completing the handshake
880     const uint64_t HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS = 30ULL * NS_PER_SEC_INT;
881     asciichat_error_t timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS);
882     if (timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
883       log_warn("Failed to set handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
884                asciichat_error_string(timeout_result));
885       // Continue anyway - timeout is a safety feature, not critical
886     }
887 
888     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_START: About to call server_crypto_handshake()");
889     int crypto_result = server_crypto_handshake(client);
890     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_DONE: result=%d", crypto_result);
891     if (crypto_result != 0) {
892       log_error("Crypto handshake failed for client %s: %s", new_client_id, network_error_string());
893       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
894         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
895       }
896       return NULL;
897     }
898 
899     // Clear socket timeout after handshake completes successfully
900     // This allows normal operation without timeouts on data transfer
901     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CLEAR_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_START");
902     asciichat_error_t clear_timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, 0);
903     if (clear_timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
904       log_warn("Failed to clear handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
905                asciichat_error_string(clear_timeout_result));
906       // Continue anyway - we can still communicate even with timeout set
907     }
908 
909     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s", new_client_id);
910 
911     // After handshake completes, the client immediately sends PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES
912     // We must read and process this packet BEFORE starting the receive thread to avoid a race condition
913     // where the packet arrives but no thread is listening for it.
914     //
915     // SPECIAL CASE: If client used --no-encrypt, we already received this packet during the handshake
916     // attempt and stored it in pending_packet_*. Use that instead of receiving a new one.
917     packet_envelope_t envelope;
918     bool used_pending_packet = false;
919 
920     if (client->pending_packet_payload) {
921       // Client used --no-encrypt mode - use the packet we already received
922       log_info("Client %s using --no-encrypt mode - processing pending packet type %u", client->client_id,
923                client->pending_packet_type);
924       envelope.type = client->pending_packet_type;
925       envelope.data = client->pending_packet_payload;
926       envelope.len = client->pending_packet_length;
927       envelope.allocated_buffer = client->pending_packet_payload; // Will be freed below
928       envelope.allocated_size = client->pending_packet_length;
929       used_pending_packet = true;
930 
931       // Clear pending packet fields
932       client->pending_packet_type = 0;
933       client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
934       client->pending_packet_length = 0;
935     } else {
936       // Normal encrypted mode - receive capabilities packet
937       log_debug("Waiting for initial capabilities packet from client %s", client->client_id);
938 
939       // Protect crypto context access with client state mutex
940       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
941       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(client->client_id);
942 
943       // Use per-client crypto state to determine enforcement
944       // At this point, handshake is complete, so crypto_initialized=true and handshake is ready
945       bool enforce_encryption = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
946                                 crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
947 
948       packet_recv_result_t result = receive_packet_secure(socket, (void *)crypto_ctx, enforce_encryption, &envelope);
949       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
950 
951       if (result != PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS) {
952         log_error("Failed to receive initial capabilities packet from client %s: result=%d", client->client_id, result);
953         if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
954           buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
955         }
956         if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
957           log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
958         }
959         return NULL;
960       }
961     }
962 
963     if (envelope.type != PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES) {
964       log_error("Expected PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES but got packet type %d from client %s", envelope.type,
965                 client->client_id);
966       if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
967         buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
968       }
969       if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
970         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
971       }
972       return NULL;
973     }
974 
975     // Process the capabilities packet directly
976     log_debug("Processing initial capabilities packet from client %s (from %s)", client->client_id,
977               used_pending_packet ? "pending packet" : "network");
978     handle_client_capabilities_packet(client, envelope.data, envelope.len);
979 
980     // Free the packet data
981     if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
982       buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
983     }
984     log_debug("Successfully received and processed initial capabilities for client %s", client->client_id);
985   }
986 
987   // Start all client threads in the correct order (unified path for TCP and WebRTC)
988   // This creates: receive thread -> render threads -> send thread
989   // The render threads MUST be created before send thread to avoid the race condition
990   // where send thread reads empty frames before render thread generates the first real frame
991   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
992   if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, true) != 0) {
993     log_error("Failed to start threads for TCP client %s", client_id_snapshot);
994     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
995     remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot);
996     return NULL;
997   }
998   log_debug("Successfully created render threads for client %s", client_id_snapshot);
999 
1000   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
1001   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
1002     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, socket, client_ip, port);
1003     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1004       log_warn("Failed to register client %s with session_host", client_id_snapshot);
1005     } else {
1006       log_debug("Client %s registered with session_host as %u", client_id_snapshot, client->session_client_id);
1007     }
1008   }
1009 
1010   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1011   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1012   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_START: About to broadcast to all clients");
1013   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1014   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_DONE");
1015 
1016   // Register client atomics for debug sync inspection
1017   register_client_info_atomics(client);
1018 
1019   log_info("[TCP_DBG] ADD_CLIENT_RETURNING: client_id=%s, client=%p", client->client_id, (void *)client);
1020   return client;
1021 
1022 error_cleanup:
1023   // Clean up all partially allocated resources
1024   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1025   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1026   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1027   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1028   return NULL;
1029 }

    client_info::incoming_video_buffer,
    client_info::is_tcp_client,
    client_info::last_rendered_grid_sources,
    client_info::last_sent_grid_sources, packet_envelope_t::len,
    log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    log_info, log_warn, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN,
    MAX_CLIENTS, MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, mixer_add_source(),
    mutex_init(), mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT, network_error_string(),
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, client_info::outgoing_video_buffer,
    packet_queue_create_with_pools(), packet_queue_destroy(),
    PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS, PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES,
    client_info::pending_packet_length,
    client_info::pending_packet_payload,
    client_info::pending_packet_type, client_info::port,
    receive_packet_secure(), remove_client(),
    rwlock_wrlock, rwlock_wrunlock, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED, safe_snprintf(), SAFE_STRERROR,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, client_info::send_buffer,
    client_info::send_buffer_size, client_info::send_mutex,
    server_crypto_handshake(), server_crypto_init(),
    client_info::server_ctx, client_info::session_client_id,
    server_context_t::session_host, session_host_add_client(),
    SET_ERRNO, set_socket_timeout(),
    client_info::shutting_down, client_info::socket,
    socket_send(), server_context_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), client_info::transport,
    packet_envelope_t::type, video_frame_buffer_create(), and
    video_frame_buffer_destroy().






client_info_t * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t
  * server_ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, const char * client_ip,
  bool start_threads)


Register a WebRTC client with the server. Registers a client that
    connected via WebRTC data channel instead of TCP socket. This function
    reuses most of add_client() logic but skips:


    
  
  • Crypto handshake (already done via ACDS signaling)
    • 
  
  • Socket-specific configuration
    • 
  
  • TCP thread pool registration
    • 



DIFFERENCES FROM add_client():


    
  
  • Takes an already-created acip_transport_t* instead of socket
    • 
  
  • No crypto handshake (WebRTC signaling handled authentication)
    • 
  
  • No socket configuration (WebRTC handles buffering)
    • 
  
  • Uses generic thread spawning instead of tcp_server thread pool
    • 



Parameters


server_ctx Server context


transport WebRTC transport (already created and connected)


client_ip Client IP address for logging (may be empty for P2P)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t on success, NULL on
  failure


Note


The transport must be fully initialized and ready to
  send/receive



Client capabilities are still expected as first packet




Definition at line 1054 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1055                                                      {
1056   if (!server_ctx || !transport || !client_ip) {
1057     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters to add_webrtc_client");
1058     return NULL;
1059   }
1060 
1061   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1062 
1063   // Find empty slot - this is the authoritative check
1064   int slot = -1;
1065   int existing_count = 0;
1066   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1067     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
1068       slot = i; // Take first available slot
1069     }
1070     // Count only active clients
1071     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
1072       existing_count++;
1073     }
1074   }
1075 
1076   // Check if we've hit the configured max-clients limit (not the array size)
1077   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients)) {
1078     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1079     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count,
1080               GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1081     log_error("Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1082     return NULL;
1083   }
1084 
1085   if (slot == -1) {
1086     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1087     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1088     log_error("No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1089     return NULL;
1090   }
1091 
1092   // Update client_count to match actual count before adding new client
1093   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count;
1094 
1095   // Generate unique client ID for WebRTC client (noun only, without counter or port)
1096   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
1097   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
1098     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1099     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID for WebRTC client");
1100     return NULL;
1101   }
1102 
1103   // Initialize client
1104   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
1105 
1106   // Free any existing buffers from previous client in this slot
1107   if (client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1108     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1109     client->incoming_video_buffer = NULL;
1110   }
1111   if (client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1112     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
1113     client->outgoing_video_buffer = NULL;
1114   }
1115   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1116     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_audio_buffer);
1117     client->incoming_audio_buffer = NULL;
1118   }
1119   if (client->send_buffer) {
1120     SAFE_FREE(client->send_buffer);
1121     client->send_buffer = NULL;
1122   }
1123 
1124   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1125 
1126   // Set up WebRTC-specific fields
1127   client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // WebRTC has no traditional socket
1128   client->is_tcp_client = false;         // WebRTC client - threads managed directly
1129   client->transport = transport;         // Use provided transport
1130   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
1131   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
1132   client->port = 0; // WebRTC doesn't use port numbers
1133   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
1134   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for receive thread cleanup
1135   log_info("Added new WebRTC client %s from %s (transport=%p, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, transport, slot);
1136   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
1137   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0); // Render thread updates this
1138   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);     // Send thread updates this
1139   log_debug("WebRTC client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d", client->client_id, slot);
1140   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
1141 
1142   // Initialize crypto context for this client
1143   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
1144   log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Setting crypto_initialized=false (will be set to true after handshake completes)");
1145   // Do NOT set crypto_initialized = true here! The handshake must complete first.
1146   // For WebSocket clients: websocket_client_handler will perform the handshake
1147   // For WebRTC clients: ACDS signaling may have already done crypto (future enhancement)
1148   client->crypto_initialized = false;
1149 
1150   // Initialize pending packet storage (unused for WebRTC, but keep for consistency)
1151   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
1152   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
1153   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
1154 
1155   // Use noun-based client name for display_name as well
1156   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->display_name, new_client_id, sizeof(client->display_name) - 1);
1157 
1158   // Create individual video buffer for this client using modern double-buffering
1159   client->incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1160   if (!client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1161     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1162     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1163     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1164   }
1165 
1166   // Create individual audio buffer for this client
1167   client->incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1168   if (!client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1169     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1170     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1171     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1172   }
1173 
1174   // Create packet queues for outgoing data
1175   client->audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
1176   if (!client->audio_queue) {
1177     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for WebRTC client");
1178     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1179   }
1180 
1181   // Create packet queue for async dispatch: received packets waiting to be processed
1182   // Capacity of 100 packets allows buffering of ~10-50 frames (depending on fragmentation)
1183   client->received_packet_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(100, 500, false);
1184   if (!client->received_packet_queue) {
1185     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create received packet queue for client");
1186     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1187   }
1188 
1189   // Create outgoing video buffer for ASCII frames (double buffered, no dropping)
1190   client->outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1191   if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1192     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for WebRTC client");
1193     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1194   }
1195 
1196   // Pre-allocate send buffer to avoid malloc/free in send thread (prevents deadlocks)
1197   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE; // 2MB should handle largest frames
1198   client->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(client->send_buffer_size, 64, void *);
1199   if (!client->send_buffer) {
1200     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1201     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1202   }
1203 
1204   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count + 1; // We just added a client
1205   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added WebRTC client_id=%s to slot %d)",
1206             g_client_manager.client_count, new_client_id, slot);
1207 
1208   // Add client to uthash table for O(1) lookup
1209   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
1210   log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
1211 
1212   // Release the write lock IMMEDIATELY after adding to hash table
1213   // All subsequent operations (mutex init, mixer registration) don't need global client manager lock
1214   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1215 
1216   // Register this client's audio buffer with the mixer
1217   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1218     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
1219       log_warn("Failed to add WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1220     } else {
1221 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1222       log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1223 #endif
1224     }
1225   }
1226 
1227   // Initialize mutexes BEFORE creating any threads to prevent race conditions
1228   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
1229     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1230     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1231     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
1232     return NULL;
1233   }
1234 
1235   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent socket writes
1236   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
1237     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1238     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1239     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1240     return NULL;
1241   }
1242 
1243   // Initialize condition variable for dispatch thread wake-up
1244   if (cond_init(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, "dispatch_queue_cond") != 0) {
1245     log_error("Failed to initialize dispatch queue condition variable for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1246     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1247     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1248     return NULL;
1249   }
1250 
1251   // For WebRTC clients, the capabilities packet will be received by the receive thread
1252   // when it starts. Unlike TCP clients where we handle it synchronously in add_client(),
1253   // WebRTC uses the transport abstraction which handles packet reception automatically.
1254   log_debug("WebRTC client %s initialized - receive thread will process capabilities", new_client_id);
1255 
1256   // Conditionally start threads based on caller preference
1257   // WebSocket handler passes start_threads=false to defer thread startup until after crypto init
1258   // This ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto context is ready
1259   if (start_threads) {
1260     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Starting client threads (receive, render) for client %s...", new_client_id);
1261     if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false) != 0) {
1262       log_error("Failed to start threads for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1263       return NULL;
1264     }
1265     log_debug("Created receive thread for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1266     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Receive thread started - thread will now be processing packets", new_client_id);
1267   } else {
1268     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Deferring thread startup for client %s (caller will start after crypto init)",
1269               new_client_id);
1270   }
1271 
1272   // Send initial server state to the new client
1273   if (send_server_state_to_client(client) != 0) {
1274     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1275   } else {
1276 #ifdef DEBUG_NETWORK
1277     log_info("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1278 #endif
1279   }
1280 
1281   // Send initial server state via ACIP transport
1282   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1283   uint32_t connected_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
1284   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1285 
1286   server_state_packet_t state;
1287   state.connected_client_count = connected_count;
1288   state.active_client_count = 0; // Will be updated by broadcast thread
1289   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
1290 
1291   // Convert to network byte order
1292   server_state_packet_t net_state;
1293   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
1294   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
1295   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
1296 
1297   asciichat_error_t packet_send_result = acip_send_server_state(client->transport, &net_state);
1298   if (packet_send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1299     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %s", new_client_id,
1300              asciichat_error_string(packet_send_result));
1301   } else {
1302     log_debug("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %u connected clients", new_client_id,
1303               state.connected_client_count);
1304   }
1305 
1306   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
1307   // WebRTC clients use INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE since they don't have a TCP socket
1308   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
1309     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, client_ip, 0);
1310     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1311       log_warn("Failed to register WebRTC client %s with session_host", new_client_id);
1312     } else {
1313       log_debug("WebRTC client %s registered with session_host as %u", new_client_id, client->session_client_id);
1314     }
1315   }
1316 
1317   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1318   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1319   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1320 
1321   return client;
1322 
1323 error_cleanup_webrtc:
1324   // Clean up all partially allocated resources for WebRTC client
1325   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1326   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1327   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1328   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1329   return NULL;
1330 }

2733   }

void
  * client_receive_thread (void * arg)


Definition at line 1793 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1793                                        {
1794   // Log thread startup
1795   log_debug("RECV_THREAD: Thread function entered, arg=%p", arg);
1796 
1797   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1798 
1799   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
1800   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
1801   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
1802   if (!client) {
1803     log_error("Invalid client info in receive thread (NULL pointer)");
1804     return NULL;
1805   }
1806 
1807   // Save this thread's ID so remove_client() can detect self-joins
1808   client->receive_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1809 
1810   log_debug("RECV_THREAD_DEBUG: Thread started, client=%p, client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d", (void *)client, client->client_id,
1811             client->is_tcp_client);
1812 
1813   if (atomic_load_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested)) {
1814     log_debug("Receive thread for client %s exiting before start (protocol disconnect requested)", client->client_id);
1815     return NULL;
1816   }
1817 
1818   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
1819   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
1820   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1821     log_debug("Receive thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
1822     return NULL;
1823   }
1824 
1825   // Additional validation: check socket is valid
1826   // For TCP clients, validate socket. WebRTC clients use DataChannel (no socket)
1827   if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1828     log_error("Invalid client socket in receive thread");
1829     return NULL;
1830   }
1831 
1832   // NOTE: Atomic registration disabled
1833   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() attempt
1834   // to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock, which conflicts with debug_sync
1835   // thread holding READ lock during printing, causing deadlock.
1836   // These are debug-only features, not critical for functionality.
1837   // The atomics work fine without being registered in the debug system.
1838 
1839   // Enable thread cancellation for clean shutdown
1840   // Thread cancellation not available in platform abstraction
1841   // Threads should exit when g_should_exit is set
1842 
1843   log_debug("Started receive thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
1844 
1845   // Main receive loop - processes packets from transport
1846   // For TCP clients: receives from socket
1847   // For WebRTC clients: receives from transport ringbuffer (via ACDS signaling)
1848 
1849   log_info("RECV_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d, transport=%p, active=%d", client->client_id,
1850            client->is_tcp_client, (void *)client->transport, atomic_load_bool(&client->active));
1851 
1852   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->active)) {
1853     // For TCP clients, check socket validity
1854     // For WebRTC clients, continue even if no socket (transport handles everything)
1855     if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1856       log_debug("TCP client %s has invalid socket, exiting receive thread", client->client_id);
1857       break;
1858     }
1859 
1860     // Check client_id is still valid before accessing transport.
1861     // This prevents accessing freed memory if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct.
1862     if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1863       log_debug("Client client_id reset, exiting receive thread");
1864       break;
1865     }
1866 
1867     // Receive packet (without dispatching) - decouple from dispatch for async processing
1868     // For WebRTC clients with async dispatch, we queue packets instead of processing immediately
1869     // This prevents backpressure on the network socket
1870 
1871     if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1872       // TCP clients: use original synchronous dispatch
1873       asciichat_error_t acip_result =
1874           acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(client->transport, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1875 
1876       // Check if shutdown was requested during the network call
1877       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
1878         log_debug("RECV_EXIT: Server shutdown requested, breaking loop");
1879         break;
1880       }
1881 
1882       // Handle receive errors
1883       if (acip_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1884         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1885         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1886           log_error("🔴 ACIP error for client %s: code=%u msg=%s", client->client_id, err_ctx.code,
1887                     err_ctx.context_message);
1888           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1889             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1890             break;
1891           } else if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_CRYPTO) {
1892             log_error_client(
1893                 client, "SECURITY VIOLATION: Unencrypted packet when encryption required - terminating connection");
1894             atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1895             break;
1896           }
1897         }
1898         log_warn("ACIP error for TCP client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1899                  asciichat_error_string(acip_result));
1900         break;
1901       }
1902     } else {
1903       // WebRTC/WebSocket clients: async dispatch - receive packet and queue for async processing
1904       void *packet_data = NULL;
1905       void *allocated_buffer = NULL;
1906       size_t packet_len = 0;
1907 
1908       // Snapshot transport pointer to prevent use-after-free
1909       // Transport can be destroyed by WebSocket callback while we're receiving
1910       acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
1911       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
1912       if (!client->transport || atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1913         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1914         log_debug("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Transport destroyed or client shutting down, exiting receive loop",
1915                   client->client_id);
1916         break;
1917       }
1918       transport_snapshot = client->transport;
1919       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1920 
1921       log_debug("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: About to call transport->recv() (transport=%p)", client->client_id,
1922                 (void *)transport_snapshot);
1923 
1924       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1925           transport_snapshot->methods->recv(transport_snapshot, &packet_data, &packet_len, &allocated_buffer);
1926 
1927       // Check error BEFORE logging - logging system may clear thread-local errno
1928       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1929         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1930         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1931           // Check for reassembly timeout (fragments arriving slowly)
1932           // This is NOT a connection failure - safe to retry
1933           if ((err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) && err_ctx.context_message &&
1934               strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "reassembly timeout")) {
1935             // Fragments are arriving slowly - this is normal, retry without disconnecting
1936             log_dev_every(100000, "Client %s: fragment reassembly timeout, retrying in 10ms", client->client_id);
1937             APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
1938             platform_sleep_ms(10); // Sleep 10ms to allow fragments to arrive
1939             continue;              // Retry without disconnecting
1940           }
1941 
1942           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1943             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1944             break;
1945           }
1946         }
1947         log_warn("Receive failed for WebRTC client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1948                  asciichat_error_string(recv_result));
1949         break;
1950       }
1951 
1952       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: transport->recv() returned result=%d, packet_len=%zu, allocated_buffer=%p",
1953                client->client_id, recv_result, packet_len, allocated_buffer);
1954 
1955       // Validate received packet before queueing
1956       if (packet_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1957         log_warn("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Received packet too small (%zu < %zu), dropping", client->client_id, packet_len,
1958                  sizeof(packet_header_t));
1959         if (allocated_buffer) {
1960           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1961         }
1962         continue;
1963       }
1964 
1965       // Queue the received packet for async dispatch
1966       // This prevents the receive thread from blocking on dispatch
1967       log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Queuing %zu byte packet for async dispatch", client->client_id, packet_len);
1968 
1969       // Extract packet type from the header to preserve it when queueing
1970       const packet_header_t *pkt_header = (const packet_header_t *)allocated_buffer;
1971       packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(pkt_header->type);
1972       uint32_t payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(pkt_header->length);
1973 
1974       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Packet header: type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1975                payload_len, packet_len);
1976 
1977       // Build a complete packet to queue (header + payload)
1978       // The entire buffer (allocated_buffer) contains the full packet
1979       // copy_data=false because we want to transfer ownership to the queue
1980       uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
1981       int enqueue_result = packet_queue_enqueue(client->received_packet_queue, pkt_type, allocated_buffer, packet_len,
1982                                                 client_id_hash, false);
1983 
1984       if (enqueue_result < 0) {
1985         log_error("🔴 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Failed to queue received packet (queue full?) - DROPPING FRAME",
1986                   client->client_id);
1987         if (allocated_buffer) {
1988           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1989         }
1990       } else {
1991         log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Successfully queued packet (type=%d, len=%zu)", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1992                  packet_len);
1993         // Signal dispatch thread to wake up and process the newly queued packet
1994         cond_signal(&client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1995       }
1996     }
1997   }
1998 
1999   // Mark client as inactive and stop all threads
2000   // Must stop render threads when client disconnects.
2001   // OPTIMIZED: Use atomic operations for thread control flags (lock-free)
2002   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
2003   log_debug("Setting active=false in receive_thread_fn (client_id=%s, exiting receive loop)", client_id_snapshot);
2004   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
2005   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2006   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
2007   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
2008 
2009   // Call remove_client() to trigger cleanup
2010   // Safe to call from receive thread now: remove_client() detects self-join via thread IDs
2011   // and skips the receive thread join when called from the receive thread itself
2012   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s calling remove_client() for cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2013   server_context_t *server_ctx = (server_context_t *)client->server_ctx;
2014   if (server_ctx) {
2015     if (remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot) != 0) {
2016       log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from receive thread cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2017     }
2018   } else {
2019     log_error("Receive thread for client %s: server_ctx is NULL, cannot call remove_client()", client_id_snapshot);
2020   }
2021 
2022   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s terminated", client_id_snapshot);
2023 
2024   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2025   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2026 
2027   return NULL;
2028 }






References acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(),
    client_info::active, APP_CALLBACK_VOID,
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_self, atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), client_info::audio_render_thread_running,
    buffer_pool_free(), client_info::client_id,
    asciichat_error_context_t::code, cond_signal(),
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    client_info::dispatch_queue_cond, client_info::display_name,
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_NETWORK, g_should_exit,
    HAS_ERRNO, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    client_info::is_tcp_client, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_dev_every, log_error,
    log_error_client, log_info, log_warn,
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,
    packet_queue_enqueue(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    client_info::protocol_disconnect_requested,
    client_info::receive_thread_id,
    client_info::received_packet_queue,
    acip_transport_methods_t::recv, remove_client(),
    client_info::send_mutex, client_info::send_thread_running,
    client_info::server_ctx, client_info::shutting_down,
    client_info::socket, client_info::transport,
    packet_header_t::type, and
    client_info::video_render_thread_running.






client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *
  client_id)


Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. This is the
    primary method for locating clients throughout the server. It uses a hash
    table for constant-time lookups regardless of client count, making it
    suitable for high-performance operations like rendering and stats.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case (hash collision)
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Hash table is internally thread-safe for lookups
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Called by render threads to find target clients for frame generation
    • 
  
  • Used by protocol handlers to locate clients for packet processing
    • 
  
  • Stats collection for per-client performance monitoring
    • 



Parameters


client_id Unique identifier for the client (0 is
  invalid)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found or invalid ID


Note


Does not require external locking - hash table provides
  thread safety



Returns direct pointer to client struct - caller should use
    snapshot pattern




Definition at line 392 of file
  src/server/client.c.


392                                                         {
393   if (!client_id || client_id[0] == '\0') {
394     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid client ID");
395     return NULL;
396   }
397 
398   // Protect uthash lookup with read lock to prevent concurrent access issues
399   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
400 
401   client_info_t *result = NULL;
402   HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, result);
403 
404   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
405 
406   if (!result) {
407     log_warn("Client not found for ID %s", client_id);
408   }
409 
410   return result;
411 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, g_client_manager,
    g_client_manager_rwlock, log_warn, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    crypto_server_cleanup_client(),
    crypto_server_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_server_encrypt_packet(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    crypto_server_is_ready(), and
  start_webrtc_client_threads().






client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t
  socket)


Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. This
    function provides socket-based client lookup, primarily used during
    connection establishment before client IDs are assigned. Less efficient than
    find_client_by_id() but necessary for socket-based operations.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of active clients
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Internally acquires read lock on
    g_client_manager_rwlock
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Connection establishment during add_client() processing
    • 
  
  • Socket error handling and cleanup operations
    • 
  
  • Debugging and diagnostic functions
    • 



Parameters


socket Platform-abstracted socket descriptor to
  search for


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found


Note


Only searches active clients (avoids returning stale
  entries)



Caller should use snapshot pattern when accessing returned client
    data




Definition at line 436 of file
  src/server/client.c.

437   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
438 
439   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
440     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].socket == socket && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
441       client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
442       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
443       return client;
444     }
445   }
446 
447   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
448   return NULL;
449 }

client Client info structure

3758     log_debug("CLIENT: client_id=%s, data=%p, len=%zu", client->client_id, data, len);
3759   }
3760 
3761   // Rate limiting: Check and record packet-specific rate limits
3762   if (g_rate_limiter) {
3763     if (!check_and_record_packet_rate_limit(g_rate_limiter, client->client_ip, client->socket, type)) {
3764       // Rate limit exceeded - error response already sent by utility function
3765       return;
3766     }
3767   }
3768 
3769   // O(1) dispatch via hash table lookup
3770   int idx = client_dispatch_hash_lookup(g_client_dispatch_hash, type);
3771   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3772     log_error("DISPATCH_LOOKUP: type=%d, idx=%d (len=%zu)", type, idx, len);
3773   }
3774   if (idx < 0) {
3775     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Unknown packet type: %d (len=%zu)", type, len);
3776     return;
3777   }
3778 
3779   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3780     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, calling handler[%d]...", type, idx);
3781   }
3782   g_client_dispatch_handlers[idx](client, data, len);
3783   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3784     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, handler returned", type);
3785   }
3786 }

Process an encrypted packet from a client




Parameters


client Client info structure


type Pointer to packet type (will be updated with decrypted type)

0 on success, -1 on error

2816   if (!crypto_server_is_ready(client->client_id)) {

2818     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2819     *data = NULL;
2820     return -1;
2821   }
2822 
2823   // Store original allocation size before it gets modified
2824   size_t original_alloc_size = *len;
2825   void *decrypted_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, original_alloc_size);
2826   size_t decrypted_len;
2827   int decrypt_result = crypto_server_decrypt_packet(client->client_id, (const uint8_t *)*data, *len,
2828                                                     (uint8_t *)decrypted_data, original_alloc_size, &decrypted_len);
2829 
2830   if (decrypt_result != 0) {
2831     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to process encrypted packet from client %s (result=%d)", client->client_id,
2832               decrypt_result);

2834     buffer_pool_free(NULL, decrypted_data, original_alloc_size);
2835     *data = NULL;
2836     return -1;
2837   }
2838 
2839   // Replace encrypted data with decrypted data
2840   // Use original allocation size for freeing the encrypted buffer
2841   buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2842 
2843   *data = decrypted_data;
2844   *len = decrypted_len;
2845 
2846   // Now process the decrypted packet by parsing its header
2847   if (*len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
2848     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Decrypted packet too small for header from client %s", client->client_id);
2849     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2850     *data = NULL;
2851     return -1;
2852   }
2853

2856   *sender_id = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->client_id);
2857 
2858   // Adjust data pointer to skip header
2859   *data = (uint8_t *)*data + sizeof(packet_header_t);
2860   *len -= sizeof(packet_header_t);
2861 
2862   return 0;
2863 }

int
  remove_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char *
  client_id)


Definition at line 1332 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1332                                                                        {
1333   if (!server_ctx) {
1334     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Cannot remove client %s: NULL server_ctx", client_id);
1335     return -1;
1336   }
1337 
1338   // Phase 1: Mark client inactive and prepare for cleanup while holding write lock
1339   client_info_t *target_client = NULL;
1340   char display_name_copy[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
1341   socket_t client_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1342 
1343   log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Attempting to remove client %s", client_id);
1344   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1345 
1346   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1347     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
1348     if (strcmp(client->client_id, client_id) == 0 && client->client_id[0] != '\0') {
1349       // Check if already being removed by another thread
1350       // This prevents double-free and use-after-free crashes during concurrent cleanup
1351       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1352         rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1353         log_debug("Client %s already being removed by another thread, skipping", client_id);
1354         return 0; // Return success - removal is in progress
1355       }
1356       // Mark as shutting down and inactive immediately to stop new operations
1357       log_debug("Setting active=false in remove_client (client_id=%s, socket=%d)", client_id, client->socket);
1358       log_info("Removing client %s (socket=%d) - marking inactive and clearing video flags", client_id, client->socket);
1359       atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
1360       atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1361       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
1362       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
1363       target_client = client;
1364 
1365       // Store display name before clearing
1366       SAFE_STRNCPY(display_name_copy, client->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
1367 
1368       // Save socket for tcp_server_stop_client_threads() before closing
1369       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1370       client_socket = client->socket; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1371       // NOTE: Do NOT call socket_shutdown() here - it sends FIN to peer and breaks the connection
1372       // The threads will exit naturally when they check shutting_down/active flags or hit errors
1373       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1374 
1375       // Shutdown packet queues to unblock send thread
1376       if (client->audio_queue) {
1377         packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
1378       }
1379       // Video now uses double buffer, no queue to shutdown
1380 
1381       break;
1382     }
1383   }
1384 
1385   // If client not found, unlock and return
1386   if (!target_client) {
1387     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1388     log_warn("Cannot remove client %s: not found", client_id);
1389     return -1;
1390   }
1391 
1392   // Unregister client from session_host (for discovery mode support)
1393   // NOTE: Client may not be registered if crypto handshake failed before session_host registration
1394   if (server_ctx->session_host && target_client->session_client_id != 0) {
1395     asciichat_error_t session_result =
1396         session_host_remove_client(server_ctx->session_host, target_client->session_client_id);
1397     if (session_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1398       // ERROR_NOT_FOUND (91) is expected if client failed crypto before being registered with session_host
1399       if (session_result == ERROR_NOT_FOUND) {
1400         log_debug("Client %s not found in session_host (likely failed crypto before registration)", client_id);
1401       } else {
1402         log_warn("Failed to unregister client %s from session_host: %s", client_id,
1403                  asciichat_error_string(session_result));
1404       }
1405     } else {
1406       log_debug("Client %s unregistered from session_host", client_id);
1407     }
1408   }
1409 
1410   // Release write lock before joining threads.
1411   // This prevents deadlock with render threads that need read locks.
1412   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1413 
1414   // Phase 2: Stop all client threads
1415   // For TCP clients: use tcp_server thread pool management
1416   // For WebRTC clients: manually join threads (no socket-based thread pool)
1417   // Use is_tcp_client flag, not socket value - socket may already be INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE
1418   // even for TCP clients if it was closed earlier during cleanup.
1419   log_debug("Stopping all threads for client %s (socket %d, is_tcp=%d)", client_id, client_socket,
1420             target_client ? target_client->is_tcp_client : -1);
1421 
1422   if (target_client && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1423     // TCP client: use tcp_server thread pool
1424     // This joins threads in stop_id order: receive(1), render(2), send(3)
1425     // Use saved client_socket for lookup (tcp_server needs original socket as key)
1426     if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1427       asciichat_error_t stop_result = tcp_server_stop_client_threads(server_ctx->tcp_server, client_socket);
1428       if (stop_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1429         log_warn("Failed to stop threads for TCP client %s: error %d", client_id, stop_result);
1430         // Continue with cleanup even if thread stopping failed
1431       }
1432     } else {
1433       log_debug("TCP client %s socket already closed, threads should have already exited", client_id);
1434     }
1435   } else if (target_client) {
1436     // WebRTC client: manually join threads
1437     log_debug("Stopping WebRTC client %s threads (receive and send)", client_id);
1438 
1439     // Join receive thread (but skip if called from the receive thread itself to avoid deadlock)
1440     thread_id_t current_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1441     if (asciichat_thread_equal(current_thread_id, target_client->receive_thread_id)) {
1442       log_debug("remove_client() called from receive thread for client %s, skipping self-join", client_id);
1443     } else {
1444       void *recv_result = NULL;
1445       asciichat_error_t recv_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->receive_thread, &recv_result);
1446       if (recv_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1447         log_warn("Failed to join receive thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, recv_join_result);
1448       } else {
1449         log_debug("Joined receive thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1450       }
1451     }
1452 
1453     // Join dispatch thread (BEFORE destroying packet queues)
1454     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->dispatch_thread)) {
1455       atomic_store_bool(&target_client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
1456       void *dispatch_result = NULL;
1457       asciichat_error_t dispatch_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->dispatch_thread, &dispatch_result);
1458       if (dispatch_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1459         log_warn("Failed to join dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, dispatch_join_result);
1460       } else {
1461         log_debug("Joined dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1462       }
1463     }
1464 
1465     // Join send thread
1466     void *send_result = NULL;
1467     asciichat_error_t send_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->send_thread, &send_result);
1468     if (send_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1469       log_warn("Failed to join send thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, send_join_result);
1470     } else {
1471       log_debug("Joined send thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1472     }
1473     // Note: Render threads still need to be stopped - they're created the same way for both TCP and WebRTC
1474     // For now, render threads are expected to exit when they check g_should_exit and client->active
1475   }
1476 
1477   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
1478   // For WebSocket clients: LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already closed and destroyed the transport
1479   // Trying to destroy it again causes heap-use-after-free since LWS callbacks might still fire
1480   // For TCP clients: transport is ours to clean up
1481   if (target_client && target_client->transport && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1482     acip_transport_destroy(target_client->transport);
1483     target_client->transport = NULL;
1484     log_debug("Destroyed ACIP transport for TCP client %s", client_id);
1485   } else if (target_client && target_client->transport && !target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1486     // WebSocket client - just NULL it out, LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already destroyed it
1487     target_client->transport = NULL;
1488     log_debug("Skipped transport destruction for WebSocket client %s (LWS already destroyed)", client_id);
1489   }
1490 
1491   // Now safe to close the socket (threads are stopped)
1492   if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1493     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Closing socket %d for client %s after thread cleanup", client_socket, client_id);
1494     socket_close(client_socket);
1495   }
1496 
1497   // Phase 3: Clean up resources with write lock
1498   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1499 
1500   // Re-validate target_client pointer after reacquiring lock.
1501   // Another thread might have invalidated the pointer while we had the lock released.
1502   if (target_client) {
1503     // Verify client_id still matches and client is still in shutting_down state
1504     bool still_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&target_client->shutting_down);
1505     if (strcmp(target_client->client_id, client_id) != 0 || !still_shutting_down) {
1506       log_warn("Client %s pointer invalidated during thread cleanup (id=%s, shutting_down=%d)", client_id,
1507                target_client->client_id, still_shutting_down);
1508       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1509       return 0; // Another thread completed the cleanup
1510     }
1511   }
1512 
1513   // Mark socket as closed in client structure
1514   if (target_client && target_client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1515     mutex_lock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1516     target_client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
1517     mutex_unlock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1518     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Client %s socket set to INVALID", target_client->client_id);
1519   }
1520 
1521   // Use the dedicated cleanup function to ensure all resources are freed
1522   cleanup_client_all_buffers(target_client);
1523 
1524   // Remove from audio mixer
1525   if (g_audio_mixer) {
1526     mixer_remove_source(g_audio_mixer, client_id);
1527 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1528     log_debug("Removed client %s from audio mixer", client_id);
1529 #endif
1530   }
1531 
1532   // Remove from uthash table
1533   // Verify client is actually in the hash table before deleting.
1534   // Another thread might have already removed it.
1535   if (target_client) {
1536     client_info_t *hash_entry = NULL;
1537     HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client->client_id, hash_entry);
1538     if (hash_entry == target_client) {
1539       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client);
1540       log_debug("Removed client %s from uthash table", client_id);
1541     } else {
1542       log_warn("Client %s already removed from hash table by another thread (found=%p, expected=%p)", client_id,
1543                (void *)hash_entry, (void *)target_client);
1544     }
1545   } else {
1546     log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from hash table (client not found)", client_id);
1547   }
1548 
1549   // Cleanup crypto context for this client
1550   if (target_client->crypto_initialized) {
1551     crypto_handshake_destroy(&target_client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
1552     target_client->crypto_initialized = false;
1553     log_debug("Crypto context cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
1554   }
1555 
1556   // Verify all threads have actually exited before resetting client_id.
1557   // Threads that are still starting (at RtlUserThreadStart) haven't checked client_id yet.
1558   // We must ensure threads are fully joined before zeroing the client struct.
1559   // Use exponential backoff for thread termination verification
1560   int retry_count = 0;
1561   const int max_retries = 5;
1562   while (retry_count < max_retries && (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->send_thread) ||
1563                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->receive_thread) ||
1564                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->video_render_thread) ||
1565                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->audio_render_thread))) {
1566     // Exponential backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
1567     uint32_t delay_ms = 10 * (1 << retry_count);
1568     log_warn("Client %s: Some threads still appear initialized (attempt %d/%d), waiting %ums", client_id,
1569              retry_count + 1, max_retries, delay_ms);
1570     platform_sleep_us(delay_ms * 1000);
1571     retry_count++;
1572   }
1573 
1574   if (retry_count == max_retries) {
1575     log_error("Client %s: Threads did not terminate after %d retries, proceeding with cleanup anyway", client_id,
1576               max_retries);
1577   }
1578 
1579   // Only reset client_id to 0 AFTER confirming threads are joined
1580   // This prevents threads that are starting from accessing a zeroed client struct
1581   // Reset client_id to NULL before destroying mutexes to prevent race conditions.
1582   // This ensures worker threads can detect shutdown and exit before the mutex is destroyed.
1583   // If we destroy the mutex first, threads might try to access a destroyed mutex.
1584   target_client->client_id[0] = '\0'; // Clear the client_id string
1585 
1586   // Wait for threads to observe the client_id reset
1587   // Use sufficient delay for memory visibility across all CPU cores
1588   platform_sleep_us(5 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 5ms delay for memory barrier propagation
1589 
1590   // Destroy mutexes and condition variables
1591   // IMPORTANT: Always destroy these even if threads didn't join properly
1592   // to prevent issues when the slot is reused
1593   mutex_destroy(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1594   mutex_destroy(&target_client->send_mutex);
1595   cond_destroy(&target_client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1596 
1597   // Clear client structure
1598   // NOTE: After memset, the mutex handles are zeroed but the OS resources
1599   // have been released by the destroy calls above
1600   memset(target_client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1601 
1602   // Recalculate client count
1603   int remaining_count = 0;
1604   for (int j = 0; j < MAX_CLIENTS; j++) {
1605     if (g_client_manager.clients[j].client_id[0] != '\0') {
1606       remaining_count++;
1607     }
1608   }
1609   g_client_manager.client_count = remaining_count;
1610 
1611   log_debug("Client removed: client_id=%s (%s) removed, remaining clients: %d", client_id, display_name_copy,
1612             remaining_count);
1613 
1614   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1615 
1616   // Broadcast updated state
1617   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1618 
1619   return 0;
1620 }

int
  start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char
  * client_id)


Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization.
    This is called by WebSocket handler after crypto handshake is initialized.
    It ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto
    context exists.




Parameters


server_ctx Server context


client_id Client ID to start threads for


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

  src/server/client.c.


2677                                                                                      {
2678   if (!server_ctx) {
2679     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Server context is NULL");
2680     return -1;
2681   }
2682 
2683   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
2684   if (!client) {
2685     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client %s not found", client_id);
2686     return -1;
2687   }
2688 
2689   log_debug("Starting threads for WebRTC client %s...", client_id);
2690   return start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false);
2691 }

void
  stop_client_threads (client_info_t * client)


Definition at line 2693 of file
  src/server/client.c.


2693                                                 {
2694   if (!client) {
2695     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
2696     return;
2697   }
2698 
2699   // Signal threads to stop
2700   log_debug("Setting active=false in stop_client_threads (client_id=%s)", client->client_id);
2701   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
2702   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2703 
2704   // Wait for threads to finish
2705   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->send_thread)) {
2706     asciichat_thread_join(&client->send_thread, NULL);
2707   }
2708   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->receive_thread)) {
2709     asciichat_thread_join(&client->receive_thread, NULL);
2710   }
2711   // For async dispatch: stop dispatch thread if running
2712   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->dispatch_thread)) {
2713     atomic_store_bool(&client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
2714     asciichat_thread_join(&client->dispatch_thread, NULL);
2715   }
2716 }

Variable
  Documentation




client_manager_t g_client_manager [extern]


Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. This
    is the primary data structure for managing all connected clients. It serves
    as the bridge between main.c's connection accept loop and the per-client
    threading architecture.




STRUCTURE COMPONENTS:


    
  
  • clients[]: Array backing storage for client_info_t structs
    • 
  
  • client_hashtable: O(1) lookup table for client_id ->
    client_info_t*
    • 
  
  • client_count: Current number of active clients
    • 
  
  • mutex: Legacy mutex (mostly replaced by rwlock)
    • 
  
  • next_client_id: Monotonic counter for unique client identification
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: Protected by g_client_manager_rwlock for concurrent
    access


Definition at line 336 of file
  src/server/client.c.






rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock [extern]


Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager. This lock
    enables high-performance concurrent access patterns:


    
  
  • Multiple threads can read client data simultaneously (stats,
    rendering)
    • 
  
  • Only one thread can modify client data at a time (add/remove
    operations)
    • 
  
  • Eliminates contention between read-heavy operations
    • 



USAGE PATTERN:


    
  
  • Read operations: rwlock_rdlock() for client lookups, stats
      gathering
    • 
  
  • Write operations: rwlock_wrlock() for add_client(),
      remove_client()
    • 
  
  • Always acquire THIS lock before per-client mutexes (lock ordering)
    • 



Definition at line 351 of file
  src/server/client.c.


351 {0};










bool g_client_manager_rwlock_initialized [extern]

    initialized.
